title: Public Relations Agencies

narrative.executive_view: Public Relations Agencies remain a real lower-middle-market acquisition universe, but the investable product is narrowing. The official NAICS definition covers campaigns, lobbying, political consulting and PR consulting [S1]; those services share a need for senior judgment and trust, while the production layer is rapidly assisted by AI. A buyer can harvest meaningful internal efficiency, but should underwrite a pressured terminal demand profile rather than a broad, durable retainer book.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI is compressing drafting, monitoring, research, media-list preparation, repurposing and reporting, not the whole client outcome. Muck Rack survey reporting says 75% of PR professionals had incorporated AI into their work by 2025 [S2], while USC documents agencies and corporate teams using AI to streamline daily work and appointing AI leadership [S3]. Crisis counsel, executive access, relationship management and accountable advice remain human-intensive, so the role estimate does not treat the profession as fully replaceable.
narrative.value_capture: AI savings initially repair unbilled over-service and improve account-team utilization. Retainer pricing can let an agency retain part of that saving, but clients see staffing and deliverables and can reset scope at renewal. The selected distribution and moat fractions are deliberately moderate: media relationships and specialization protect a portion of price, whereas generic release, content and reporting work faces visible deflation. This is a capture judgment, not a claim that retained hours establish terminal demand.
narrative.adoption_timing: The 75% professional-use survey measure is above the scorecard's 50% effective-adoption threshold, so t50 is zero by the frozen rule [S2]. It is not a NAICS 541820 establishment census and does not prove every workflow is automated. The high usage reading nevertheless makes waiting for a distant adoption inflection implausible; implementation quality and client policy, rather than access to tools, are the limiting variables.
narrative.consolidation_economics: PRovoke tracked 80 PR-sector deals in 2025 and reported a tilt toward larger, integrated sellers; it also identifies PE-backed buyers and data/technology capability as a value driver [S5]. That supports active strategic interest but is not proof that a $1-10M EBITDA generalist agency has the same demand. Founder-linked books remain central: a PR-agency transaction guide describes 60-70% cash at close and 30-40% earnouts tied to client and senior-publicist retention [S4], making headline consideration a weak proxy for immediately transferable EBITDA.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand should survive in specialist, regulated, crisis and high-stakes corporate communications, but the broad generalist product is pressured. Muck Rack's survey reporting highlights media relations as the leading job function and respondents cite low journalist response rates and shrinking newsrooms as obstacles [S2]. That is a non-AI headwind layered on AI's removal of routine production. The likely endpoint is fewer paid senior-counsel hours per account, not simply the same retainer delivered at a lower cost.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The deterministic role mix is a 4-digit 5418 proxy that includes advertising and media-representation occupations, so the role model uses a large explicitly uncertain residual. No NAICS-specific owner-age statistic or private lower-middle-market PR multiple database was available in the reopened sources. The multiple is a broad marketing-agency proxy and the ownership, consolidator and capture estimates should be diligence items, especially client concentration, founder dependence, earnout mechanics, renewal language and specialty mix.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS United States Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official 541820 scope: establishments designing and implementing PR campaigns, including lobbying, political consulting and PR consulting.

sources.title: Communicators embrace tools like AI and LinkedIn at rapid pace
sources.what_it_supports: Muck Rack survey reporting on PR professionals' AI use, media-relations work and newsroom/response-rate headwinds.

sources.title: Study reveals rising application of AI across communications by the public relations industry
sources.what_it_supports: USC Annenberg description of agency and corporate AI application in communications work.

sources.title: Buy a PR Agency
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial transaction guide describing PR-agency client-relationship transfer risk and typical earnout structure.

sources.title: 2025 M&A Review: Fewer Deals But Bigger Deals
sources.what_it_supports: PR-sector 2025 deal activity, larger-seller concentration, PE activity and buyer demand for technology/data capability.

sources.title: Marketing Agency EBITDA Multiples & Valuations – 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial broad marketing-agency EBITDA multiple table, segmented by EBITDA size, used only as a disclosed valuation proxy.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541820 ($9,020,215,177) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541820 ($16,903,608,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $271,021 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541820); class <5: 6,209 firms, avg revenue/firm $468,889 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $73,381 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 927 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,829,861 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $286,373 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 551 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,315,470 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $518,871 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 348 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,294,121 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,611,030 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 73 firms, avg revenue/firm $38,745,397 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,063,655 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 49 firms, avg revenue/firm $82,824,510 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $12,962,036 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 421
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI use is already widespread in PR, but role fractions retain senior counsel, judgment and relationship work. The 4-digit occupational proxy requires an explicit residual estimate.
caveats: OEWS role mix is 4-digit 5418 and includes advertising/media-rep contamination rather than a pure 541820 workforce.
Residual roles are not observed and drive much of the estimate.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Pitch preparation and CRM support automate; relationship-led selling remains.
Drafting, research and monitoring automate; counsel and journalist relationships remain.

breakdown_by_role.role: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Research, analysis and reporting are highly AI-assisted.
Internal management automates more than senior client judgment.
Production design automates, while brand judgment remains.
This is likely proxy contamination; only limited automation is credited.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposals and updates automate; relationship ownership remains.
Likely proxy contamination, therefore conservatively treated.
Planning support automates, strategic accountability remains.
Concept iteration automates more than direction and approval.
Unlisted delivery, administration and account-support roles mix automatable workflow with client service.

pi_dist.rationale: Retainer and earnout structures indicate relationship-dependent, continuing accounts; savings can be retained initially but client renewal and scope reset constrain pass-through retention.
caveats: No reopened source provides a NAICS-specific measurement of AI savings pass-through.

pi_moat.rationale: Senior publicist retention, specialty capability and client relationships protect part of pricing, while generic production is contestable.
caveats: Protection varies sharply by crisis, regulated and generalist subsegment.

t50_years.rationale: The observed 75% PR-professional AI-use measure exceeds 50%, requiring zero under the frozen threshold rule.
caveats: Professional use is a proxy for effective adoption at NAICS 541820 establishments.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Axios reports Muck Rack's finding that three in four PR professionals had incorporated AI into their work.
caveats: Survey respondents are PR professionals, not a random US NAICS-541820 establishment sample.

historical_analogs.value: Digitization of media monitoring and social publishing lowered production effort while premium counsel remained relationship-led; generative AI extends that pattern into drafting and research.
historical_analogs.rationale: The sources describe adoption in everyday communications work, supporting the workflow analogy.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Routine-production compression with variable senior-counsel retention
caveats: This is an explanatory analogy, not an observed financial series.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: Founder-linked relationship transfer and transition requirements support a meaningful owner-dependence estimate, not an observed age statistic.
caveats: No NAICS-specific owner-age study was found in reopened sources.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: The reopened sources show transition risk but do not document a NAICS-specific succession shortage.
caveats: Absence of a reopened source is not evidence that no shortage exists.

active_consolidators.rationale: PRovoke identifies active PE-backed and strategic transaction activity; three represents a conservative count of relevant buyer categories, not a complete buyer census.
caveats: The source covers sector transactions, including larger agencies and non-US activity.

buy_mult.rationale: A lower-end broad marketing-agency proxy is selected for a founder-dependent $1-10M EBITDA PR target.
caveats: S6 is a commercial broad marketing-agency table, not disclosed NAICS-541820 private transactions; earnouts can make headline multiple non-cash-at-close.

exit_mult.rationale: A modest 0.5x step-up assumes specialization, retained team and data capability, consistent with buyer preferences, without assuming broad multiple expansion.
caveats: Multiple support is broad-sector and must be replaced with process-specific indications in diligence.

pricing_structure.value: Monthly retainers and project fees tied to client relationships, scoped deliverables and senior-counsel access.
pricing_structure.rationale: The transaction guide's client-retention earnout emphasis supports relationship-dependent recurring service pricing.
caveats: No NAICS-wide pricing survey was reopened.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Immediate AI use supports rapid availability of operating savings, but the estimate does not assume adoption creates higher capture.

terminal_value.justification: Senior counsel survives, but AI compresses routine production and newsroom/response-rate headwinds reduce the breadth of the earned-media product [S2].

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No; the 4-digit occupation mix, professional adoption survey and broad marketing multiple table are disclosed proxies.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Retainer savings can first reduce over-service; renewal, staffing-plan visibility and generic content alternatives can then compress fees.

dataset_mismatch.description: The supplied 4-digit 5418 role mix includes advertising and media-representation occupations and is not a pure PR-agency census.
sensitivity: The large residual role judgment materially affects V.
Reclassifying contaminant roles could move the replaceable-share estimate within its stated range.

market_boundary.selection_basis: Official 541820 includes PR campaigns, lobbying, political consulting and PR consulting [S1]; the thesis is therefore a broad-code view.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: 0 under the reported professional-use threshold; workflow-level effective adoption may lag
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A remains 10 at zero, so a lower effective-adoption interpretation would reduce A.
Moves V materially because deterministic labor share is high.
Moves M and requires real target-specific bids.

reviewer_note: Initial fleet packet: all cited score-driving URLs were reopened on 2026-07-21; estimates carry explicit source/proxy caveats.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=0, A=10
